name: Chelo Seebzamini (Steamed Rice with Saffron P
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Imported Iranian rice, or substitute other white rice -- soaked and drained
1 small baking potato, peeled and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy 3 to 4 quart casserole with a tightly fitting lid, bring 6 cups water to a boil over high heat.
2. Add rice in a slow, thin stream so the water does not stop boiling. Stir once or twice, then boil briskly for 5 minutes.
3. Drain the rice in a sieve.
4. Meanwhile, mash saffron and sugar together to a powder using a mortar and pestle. Stir in the melted butter.
5. Add the sliced potatoes and turn them with a spoon until thoroughly coated.
6. Spread the potatoes out flat so they cover the entire bottom of the casserole and spoon the rice over them, mounding slightly in the center.
7. Dot the top of the rice with the remaining 2 tbsp butter.
8. Cover tightly and cook over high heat for 5 minutes.
9. Place a sheet of aluminum foil over the casserole to seal it completely and set the lid back in place.
10. Reduce the heat to the lowest possible point and steam the rice and potatoes for 45 minutes, or until tender.
11. To serve, spoon the rice in a mound on a heated serving platter. Gently lift out the potato slices and arrange them browned side up on the rice.
12. Chelo Seebzamini is traditionally served with a khoresh or stew.
